title: Add 'MOD x RDX' branding text and update UI color to #7B2EFF

requirements.summary: Add 'MOD x RDX' text in bold sans-serif font next to the icon.png logo on the left side of the main page, ensuring horizontal alignment
acceptanceCriteria: The text 'MOD x RDX' appears next to the circular logo on the homepage
The text uses a bold sans-serif font family
The text and logo are horizontally aligned
The layout remains responsive on all screen sizes

file_operations.description: Add 'MOD x RDX' text in bold sans-serif font next to the icon.png logo. Wrap the logo image and text in a flex container with horizontal alignment (items-center). Apply font-bold and sans-serif font family to the text element. Ensure the container is responsive across all screen sizes.

requirements.summary: Add 'MOD x RDX' text in bold sans-serif font next to the icon.png logo in the mobile sheet menu navigation, ensuring horizontal alignment
acceptanceCriteria: The text 'MOD x RDX' appears next to the circular logo in the mobile sheet menu
The text uses a bold sans-serif font family
The text and logo are horizontally aligned within the mobile menu
The mobile menu layout remains clean and functional

file_operations.description: Add 'MOD x RDX' text in bold sans-serif font next to the icon.png logo within the mobile sheet menu. Wrap the logo and text in a flex container with horizontal alignment (items-center). Apply font-bold and sans-serif font family to the text element. Maintain clean mobile menu layout and functionality.

requirements.summary: Update the website background and UI color scheme to use #7B2EFF by modifying root CSS variables for both light and dark modes
acceptanceCriteria: The background color uses #7B2EFF throughout the site
The color scheme is consistent across all pages
Both light and dark mode configurations use the #7B2EFF color appropriately
Text remains readable against the new background color
The color applies to relevant UI elements (background, cards, borders, etc.)

file_operations.description: Update the root CSS variables in both light and dark mode configurations to use #7B2EFF (converted to OKLCH format). Modify background, primary, accent, and other relevant color tokens to incorporate the purple color while ensuring text readability. Apply the color scheme consistently to backgrounds, cards, borders, and UI elements throughout the application.
